paper: Verifiable Multi-Agent Reasoning for LLM Agents in Zero-Communication Mixed-Motive Games

responses.likelyConcern: Is this just a Guandan bot or a game-specific prompting pipeline?
responses.currentResponse: The contribution is a verifier-grounded diagnostic framework for structured LLM reasoning under zero communication; Guandan is the dense imperfect-information testbed, not the claimed product.
responses.scopeBoundary: The paper does not claim stronger Guandan play, win-rate improvement, or a general game-solving policy.
responses.requiredAction: Keep the contribution framed as verifiable reasoning diagnostics under zero communication, not as a stronger card-playing agent.

responses.likelyConcern: Are the results specific to one provider, one model, or one prompting stack?
responses.currentResponse: The current artifacts include a completed primary ToM full split and a ready second-provider pilot package, but independent provider/model outputs are not yet paper evidence.
responses.scopeBoundary: Do not claim provider robustness until second-provider/model outputs return and are ingested.
responses.requiredAction: Complete the prepared human soft-label audit, or add a second model/provider pilot replication; ideally do both before claiming robust multi-agent reasoning behavior.

responses.likelyConcern: Are the gains merely schema formatting rather than improved reasoning validity?
responses.currentResponse: The paper separates raw parse yield, deterministic schema repair, paired verifier revision, and hard-failure attribution; the attribution gate specifically isolates public-history and hidden-information repairs.
responses.scopeBoundary: Schema repair is reported as reliability plumbing; paired hard-label changes are the reasoning-validity evidence.
responses.requiredAction: Use this as a reviewer-facing defense against the claim that all gains are formatting gains.

responses.likelyConcern: Does the verifier act as an oracle or silently choose better actions?
responses.currentResponse: The verifier maps a fixed state, trace, and selected action to labels and issue codes; revision repairs commitments under the same decision id and the manuscript explicitly separates reasoning validity from optimal play.
evidence: Limitations and reviewer-boundary text present: yes
responses.scopeBoundary: Verifier labels diagnose validity and evidence boundaries; they are not strategic optimality labels and not full-game outcome claims.
responses.requiredAction: Keep the limitation language: verifier labels diagnose reasoning validity, not optimal play or cross-game transfer.

responses.likelyConcern: Are parse failures hidden by reporting only the favorable paired subset?
responses.currentResponse: The paper exposes provider completion, parse yield, schema failures, paired revision denominator, and provenance tables; paired analysis is restricted to eligible traces but end-to-end failures remain visible.
responses.scopeBoundary: The 32-trace paired result must always be read alongside 50-decision end-to-end accounting.
responses.requiredAction: Keep Table 4/Figure 4 denominator language and provenance table wording intact after every edit.

responses.likelyConcern: Is the contribution novel relative to LLM coordination, Hanabi, ToM-Guandan, ToolPoker, and process-aware mixed-motive evaluation?
responses.currentResponse: The manuscript positions the paper narrowly around deterministic checking of structured traces in zero-explicit-communication, mixed-motive, dynamic-action team play.
responses.scopeBoundary: The paper must not claim to introduce Guandan, ToM prompting, LLM game agents, or action-space reduction.
responses.requiredAction: Keep related-work limitations explicit and refresh novelty checks before final submission.

responses.likelyConcern: Are the soft partner/opponent labels subjective or tuned to the authors expectations?
responses.currentResponse: A blind human-audit packet and annotator package exist, but no returned labels or agreement metrics are available yet; this remains an external-evidence gap.
responses.scopeBoundary: Treat soft labels as conservative diagnostics until human agreement evidence is complete.
responses.requiredAction: Run the fixed second-provider pilot replication when API access is available, or complete the prepared human audit packet.

responses.likelyConcern: Can the paper fit a full AAMAS submission without looking like a protocol dump?
responses.currentResponse: The AAMAS-style PDF remains 9 pages total with the main body ending on page 8, and the visual package provides five role-covered figures and nine compact tables.
responses.scopeBoundary: New evidence should replace protocol scaffolding rather than expanding the 8-page body.
responses.requiredAction: Preserve the 8-page body budget by replacing protocol/scaffolding material when adding full-split results.

responses.likelyConcern: Are the reported numbers, figures, and tables reproducible from local artifacts?
responses.currentResponse: The finalizer, manifest, local pipeline, method reproducibility report, and claim-evidence report are present; local artifact hygiene passes with no missing manifest entries.
responses.scopeBoundary: Reproducibility covers current local artifacts, not unavailable second-provider or returned human-audit evidence.
responses.requiredAction: Run the finalizer after every manuscript, experiment, or figure change.

nextActions: Complete the prepared human soft-label audit, or add a second model/provider pilot replication; ideally do both before claiming robust multi-agent reasoning behavior.
Run the fixed second-provider pilot replication when API access is available, or complete the prepared human audit packet.
Decide whether to add full-split plain/candidate LLM baselines or keep them as optional strengthening evidence.
Preserve the 8-page body budget by replacing protocol/scaffolding material if more evidence moves into the main paper.
